ddls:
CREATE TABLE "environmental_data" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was recorded */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Recorded temperature value */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the data was collected */,
  "sensor_id" TEXT /* ID of the sensor that recorded the data */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Recorded humidity value */,
  "air_quality" REAL /* Recorded air quality value */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device used for logging */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Firmware version of the device used for logging */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "image_data" (
  "image_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each image */,
  "image_file" TEXT /* File path to the image */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the image was captured */,
  "read_time" REAL /* Time taken to read the image */,
  "predict_time" REAL /* Time taken to predict objects in the image */,
  "objects_detected" INTEGER /* Number of objects detected in the image */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the image was captured */,
  "camera_id" TEXT /* ID of the camera that captured the image */,
  "image_size" TEXT /* Size of the image */,
  "image_resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the image */,
  "image_format" TEXT /* Format of the image */,
  "contains_sensitive_info" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the image contains sensitive information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("image_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "object_detection" (
  "detection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each detection */,
  "image_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the image in which the object was detected */,
  "object_type" TEXT /* Type of object detected */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the detection */,
  "bounding_box_coordinates" TEXT /* Coordinates of the bounding box for the detected object */,
  "detection_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the object was detected */,
  "image_size" TEXT /* Size of the image */,
  "image_resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("detection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_object_detection_image_id FOREIGN KEY ("image_id") REFERENCES image_data ("image_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, city official, public user) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the user (e.g., organization, department) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "data_size" TEXT /* Size of the data accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES environmental_data ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "image_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "image_id" INTEGER /* ID of the image being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the image */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the image was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "image_size" TEXT /* Size of the image accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_image_access_logs_image_id FOREIGN KEY ("image_id") REFERENCES image_data ("image_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_image_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the setting was last updated */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the setting */,
  "setting_group" TEXT /* Category of the setting (e.g., environmental, security) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
